focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 30、INT. PARLOR, NOLAN HOUSE - THAT NIGHT,
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: An Old Woman examines Benjamin, joking he resembles her ex-husband; Queenie reveals his poor health, prompting somber laughter that evokes the baby's tragic, aged smile.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Benjamin is introduced to the residents of Nolan House and informally taken in by Queenie for care.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: accepted into Nolan House under Queenie's care, recognized as prematurely aged and fragile
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: limited life expectancy and uncertain future
evidence_quotes: He's prematurely old... Doctor Rose said he don't have much time on this earth...
God in heaven, he looks just like my ex-husband...
Their laughter makes the baby seem to smile... the lonely smile of an old, dying man.

relation_updates: accepted by Queenie
introduced to the Old People

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 34、INT. THE NOLAN HOUSE - DINING ROOM, NEW ORLEANS -
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Benjamin Button, an old-looking child with age-related ailments, behaves like a child while eating but is treated like an elderly man among peers, following instructions and receiving care.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Benjamin is treated as an elderly peer by the household and follows instructions like the other older residents, despite displaying childlike behavior during dinner.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Benjamin is integrated into the household as an old man, complying with routines and receiving care alongside the elderly boarders.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: dissonance between physical age and mental/behavioral youth
evidence_quotes: He's sitting in a wheelchair now...
he uses his fork like a child might, banging it just for the hell of it making noise...
Stop bangin' that fork... (fixing it in his arthritic hand) It's for eating, not for playin' with...
And he does as he's told...

relation_updates: treated as peer by elderly boarders
receives caregiving from Queenie as authority figure
status_updates: physically aged with arthritis, hearing aid, eyeglasses

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 42、INT. CHURCH TENT, NEW ORLEANS - A SATURDAY NIGHT,
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Benjamin witnesses the preacher's sudden death from a coronary, as nurses rush to the lifeless body on stage, deepening his reflection on the fragility of life and faith.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: child with physical limitations undergoing a faith-based healing attempt
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Benjamin attempts to walk across the church stage under the preacher's command, struggling but eventually hobbling forward with assistance from Queenie and the preacher.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to walk despite physical limits
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: manages to hobble across the stage with support, receiving enthusiastic applause and affirmation from the congregation
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: physical fragility and inability to stand or walk independently remain evident
evidence_quotes: And Benjamin, doing what he's asked, barely able to, his legs akimbo, stands...
And Benjamin takes two very precarious steps and his poor arthritic legs give out... and he sprawls to the floor...
And Benjamin, slowly but surely, makes his way to his feet...
And Benjamin, one crippled leg at a time, hobbles across the stage...

relation_updates: strengthened bond with Queenie and preacher
auxiliary.persona_anchor: perseveres through physical difficulty when encouraged by faith and authority figures

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 49、INT. DINING ROOM, NOLAN HOUSE - ANOTHER DAY,
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Benjamin, despite warnings and hesitation, follows Mr. Oti outside, leaving his safe routine behind in a quiet but decisive step into the unknown, marked by tension and change.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Benjamin defies warnings and follows Mr. Oti outside, leaving his routine to pursue an uncertain connection.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: seeking connection beyond safety
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: steps outside the house with Mr. Oti, entering the public world for the first time with deliberate intent
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: risk of exposure and consequences of leaving the safety of the house
evidence_quotes: then casually suggests, 'You want to get a cold root beer?'
Benjamin hesitates, watching Queenie lean out a window, beating dust from a rug, a familiar scene anchoring him to safety and routine.
Despite knowing the consequences, Benjamin takes up his canes and follows Ngunda Oti outside, stepping into the unknown.
The door closes behind him, leaving the house still, the air charged with implication.

relation_updates: begins to trust Mr. Oti despite uncertainty
auxiliary.persona_anchor: driven by curiosity and emerging trust

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 73、EXT. THE MISSISSIPPI RIVER - DAY,
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Benjamin eagerly takes a job on Capt. Mike's tugboat despite warnings about unpaid wages, proving willing to work when others hesitate.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Benjamin takes a job on Captain Mike's tugboat despite warnings that the captain never pays, showing his willingness to work when others refuse.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: seeking purpose and independence through labor
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: begins working as a crew member on a tugboat, entering the workforce and taking on adult responsibilities
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: the job offers no guaranteed pay, raising concerns about financial stability
evidence_quotes: A MAN (warning them) He never pays... He always says he'll have to owe it to you...
Benjamin suddenly springs up at the opportunity, waving his arms... BENJAMIN I do...!
CAPT. MIKE You got your sea legs old man?
BENJAMIN (feeling his legs) I do. I think.

relation_updates: forms working relationship with Captain Mike
status_updates: gains role as crew member on a tugboat

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 75、INT. TUGBOAT WHEELHOUSE - END OF ANOTHER DAY,
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Captain Mike, shocked by Benjamin's admission of never having been with a woman, invites him along to meet some friends, prompting a new experience.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Benjamin admits he has never been with a woman, prompting Captain Mike to take him to meet some friends.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Benjamin becomes the subject of Captain Mike's impromptu mentorship regarding personal experience and intimacy
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: lack of intimate experience and emotional connection
evidence_quotes: Never.
Well, then, hell man, you comin' with me!!
He took me to meet some friends...

relation_updates: Captain Mike shifts from employer to mentor figure
status_updates: his innocence and isolation are exposed

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 85、INT. THE BAR - FRENCH QUARTER - LATER THAT NIGHT,
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Thomas reveals he makes buttons, competes with zippers; Benjamin, a tugboat worker, offers to pay for another drink, showing pride in his work.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Benjamin and Thomas exchange personal losses and discuss their work, creating a moment of mutual recognition and emotional connection.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: seeking connection and validation through shared experience
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Benjamin and Thomas have established a deeper bond through the exchange of personal grief and pride in their labor, marked by a toast and Benjamin's offer to pay for a drink.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Thomas hesitates to reveal more about his past after mentioning his wife's death, indicating unspoken history.
evidence_quotes: My wife passed away many years ago... She died in childbirth.
To children,
To mothers and fathers,
clinking glasses in shared sentiment.

relation_updates: reciprocal disclosure of personal loss and work life

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 159、INT. THE NOLAN HOUSE - EARLY ONE EVENING,
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Benjamin reunites with Daisy, now a woman, expressing awe and regret for stopping his letters, acknowledging her transformation and his lingering feelings.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: returning sailor reuniting with childhood friend
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Benjamin reunites with Daisy after years apart, recognizing her transformation into a woman and experiencing a shift in their emotional connection.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: reconnect with Daisy
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Benjamin is emotionally affected by Daisy's presence, aware of her changed appearance and the new intimacy between them.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Daisy expresses lingering hurt over Benjamin stopping his letters.
evidence_quotes: When I had left she was a girl... and a woman had taken her place...
They embrace, and there's a moment when the touch is not just of old friends, but something different...
DAISY You stopped writing.

relation_updates: relationship with Daisy becomes emotionally charged and intimate
auxiliary.persona_anchor: love persists across time and change

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 171、INT. WORK FLOOR, BUTTON FACTORY - NEW ORLEANS - NIGHT
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Thomas Button reveals to Benjamin that he is his long-abandoned son, born at the end of the great war, and that he left him as a baby, fearing he was a monster, but now seeks connection.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Thomas Button reveals he is Benjamin's biological father and admits to abandoning him as an infant out of fear, leading Benjamin to learn the truth about his parentage.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Benjamin is emotionally stunned and silent upon learning that Thomas is his father and that his mother died in childbirth.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Benjamin's struggle to process the revelation of his father's abandonment and the truth of his origins
evidence_quotes: THOMAS BUTTON You are my family. Benjamin, you are my son.
Benjamin is still.
BENJAMIN My mother?

relation_updates: biological father-son relationship confirmed
status_updates: emotional bond redefined by truth and betrayal
auxiliary.persona_anchor: identity shaped by abandonment and resilience

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 215、INT. THE BEDROOM, NOLAN HOUSE - THAT NIGHT,
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Benjamin and Daisy, undressing and kissing passionately, share intimate moments as she reveals fragments of her past; he hesitates, reassures her, kisses her scarred leg, and they make love in a silent, home-movie-like scene.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Benjamin and Daisy share an intimate moment, undressing and kissing before making love, during which she reveals fragments of her past and he kisses her scarred leg.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: seeking emotional closeness
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: emotionally exposed and physically intimate
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: unspoken concerns about their future due to Benjamin's condition
evidence_quotes: DAISY Sleep with me.
And they kiss, A kiss that has waited for thirty years.
While he gently, tenderly, kisses her, undresses her...
He kisses her scarred crooked leg.

relation_updates: intimacy deepens
mutual vulnerability established

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 257、EXT. A PARK - NEW ORLEANS - DAY,
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Daisy fears Benjamin is leaving her as she questions her aging and his feelings, while Benjamin remains silent, reflecting on a past birthday celebration.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Benjamin tells Daisy she should find a real father for Caroline, indicating his intention to depart due to his atypical aging.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to release Daisy and Caroline from a future he cannot fulfill
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Benjamin remains silent after Daisy pleads with him not to leave, confirming his unspoken decision to go
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: how to leave without causing further pain
evidence_quotes: BENJAMIN She needs someone to grow old with...
BENJAMIN She needs a father not a playmate.
DAISY You're going to leave me, aren't you? He doesn't say anything.

relation_updates: emotional withdrawal from Daisy
conflicted paternal role toward Caroline
status_updates: transition from partner to temporary caregiver

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 291、INT. BENJAMIN AND DAISY'S ROOM, NOLAN HOUSE - DAY,
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Benjamin closes his eyes and passes away peacefully in Daisy's lap, his stillness marking the end of his journey as she holds him.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Benjamin dies in Daisy's lap, having regressed to an infant, but recognizing her in his final moments through eye contact and holding her thumb.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Benjamin is deceased, physically reduced to a newborn-like state, his life concluded in Daisy's presence.
evidence_quotes: He looked into my eyes... And we see him looking into her eyes...
he moved his fingers... and with his little thumb he held my thumb...
at that moment I knew... he knew who I was...
he closed his eyes like he was sleeping... And we see his eyes flutter and softly close... forever.

relation_updates: Daisy confirms Benjamin recognizes her despite physical regression
status_updates: Benjamin physically regressed to near-newborn state
auxiliary.persona_anchor: life defined by connection, not time

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Benjamin Button is born with the appearance of an elderly man and is raised in a New Orleans nursing home, where he forms deep bonds with Queenie and the residents. As he physically regresses while aging mentally, he seeks connection, love, and purpose through relationships with Daisy, Elizabeth Abbott, and Captain Mike, and through work and travel. His reverse aging creates emotional asymmetries that shape his decisions, leading to a bittersweet reunion and eventual separation from Daisy. He dies peacefully in her arms, having lived a life defined by fleeting but meaningful connections and a quiet acceptance of time's passage.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 1、INT. HOSPITAL ROOM, NEW ORLEANS - MORNING, PRESENT
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Daisy Fuller, nearing death, shares a final moment with her daughter Caroline, who expresses gratitude and love, while caregiver Dorothy administers morphine; Daisy recalls a memory of her father at a train station's opening.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Caroline expresses gratitude and love to her dying mother, seeking emotional closure in their final moments together.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: seeking closure before her mother's death
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: emotionally vulnerable but comforted by expressing her feelings
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: unspoken emotional distance remains between mother and daughter
evidence_quotes: Caroline, seeking closure, sits on the bed with her and starts to cry.
I wanted to thank you, Mother, for bringing me into this world. For raising me so well.
I wanted to tell you how much you've meant to me. I'm going to miss you so much...

relation_updates: emotional reconciliation with mother

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 18、INT. HOSPITAL ROOM, NEW ORLEANS - MORNING, PRESENT
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Caroline reads her mother Daisy's journal, beginning a story dated April 4, 1985, from New Orleans, written by someone named Benjamin Button who recounts being born under unusual circumstances.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Caroline expresses regret about her life choices and fears disappointing her mother; Daisy responds with reassurance and dismisses conventional expectations.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: seeking validation for her life choices
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: emotionally exposed but soothed by her mother's words and presence
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: absence of grandchildren and feeling out of sync with traditional life milestones
evidence_quotes: DAISY Oh honey, you could never disappoint me.
DAISY What's normal? A hat full of sand.

relation_updates: receives maternal reassurance

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 32、INT. THE HOSPITAL ROOM, NEW ORLEANS - DAY, PRESENT
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Caroline finds a torn journal with a streetcar token, questions its truth, and gives the token to her distracted mother, Daisy, who fixates on a clock.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Caroline finds a streetcar token inside the journal and questions its truth, then physically connects with her mother by placing the token in her hand.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: trying to understand the journal's significance
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: skeptical but increasingly engaged with the journal and its possible connection to her family
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: uncertainty about the journal's authenticity and missing pages
evidence_quotes: CAROLINE Is any of this true?
CAROLINE Some pages seem to be torn out here...
She discovers inside... CAROLINE (CONT'D) There's an old streetcar token.
She gives it to her mother, folding her hand around it.

relation_updates: physically connects with mother through token
status_updates: becomes curious about the journal's authenticity

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 64、INT. THE HOSPITAL ROOM, NEW ORLEANS - DAY, PRESENT
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Daisy lies dying as Caroline reflects on unrequited love; Benjamin recalls a changed relationship after a baby's arrival, linked to a rewritten note.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Caroline expresses her sense of never having been loved deeply, in contrast to her mother's romantic past, during a quiet hospital visit.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: seeking emotional closure through her mother's story
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: acknowledges her own emotional isolation in the moment
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: desire for profound love remains unfulfilled
evidence_quotes: Nobody has ever loved me that way.
This man. He loved you from the first time he saw you.
Are you alright, Mother?
He crossed out something... and then he's written... "When..."

relation_updates: reinforces emotional distance in mother-daughter relationship

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 72、INT. HOSPITAL ROOM, NEW ORLEANS - DAY, PRESENT
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Daisy briefly opens her eyes, sharing a quiet moment with Caroline, who begins reading before Daisy closes her eyes as Benjamin's voice narrates a memory of boat trips with Mr. Daws.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Caroline acknowledges she has not spent as much time with Daisy in recent years as she would have liked, expressing regret during Daisy's final hours.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: making amends through presence
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: present and attentive at Daisy's bedside, engaging in quiet care
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: regret over past absence in Daisy's life
evidence_quotes: CAROLINE I haven't spent as much time as I would have like with her the last few years.

relation_updates: acknowledges strained history with mother
status_updates: takes on caretaker role during final hours

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 186、INT. HOSPITAL ROOM, NEW ORLEANS - MORNING, PRESENT
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Daisy, wearing a turban, whispers to Caroline while she reads, admitting she knew she would hurt her and may have intended to.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Daisy whispers to Caroline that she knew she would hurt her and may have intended to, introducing a revelation of possible deliberate emotional harm.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Caroline is left silently absorbing Daisy's admission of potential intentional harm, marking a moment of emotional weight and relational strain.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: the impact of Daisy's confession is not addressed or discussed in the moment.
evidence_quotes: listening to Caroline read
I knew I'd hurt you... Maybe I intended to...

relation_updates: target of Daisy's harmful intent
figure of emotional vulnerability
status_updates: present during Daisy's moment of guilt

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 190、INT. HOSPITAL ROOM, NEW ORLEANS - MORNING, PRESENT
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Daisy recalls her peak dancing years, performing globally and being the only American with the Bolshoi, while Caroline discovers unseen photos of her.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Caroline finds photographs of Daisy's dancing career and learns about her international performances, including with the Bolshoi.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Caroline becomes aware of Daisy's accomplished dance career, which she had not previously known about.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Daisy's emotional detachment from her past suggests unresolved feelings about her career.
evidence_quotes: Caroline goes to the suitcases... she comes back with a manila envelope...
I've never seen these... You never talked about your dancing very much...

relation_updates: gains new understanding of Daisy's past
status_updates: learns about Daisy's international dance career

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 253、INT. HOSPITAL ROOM, NEW ORLEANS - MORNING, PRESENT
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Caroline learns Benjamin was her father, confronts Daisy for never revealing the truth, and leaves upset; Daisy reflects on their shared loss.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Caroline learns Benjamin was her biological father, confronts Daisy about the secrecy, and leaves the room visibly upset.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: seeks truth about her origins
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: emotionally overwhelmed, exits the room to be alone
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: has not reconciled with Daisy or processed the implications of her father's identity
evidence_quotes: CAROLINE He was my father...? This Benjamin was my father?
Why didn't you ever tell me...?!
CAROLINE You never said a word to me...
Caroline, upset, gets up...

relation_updates: discovers and confronts her biological father's identity
relationship with Daisy strained by revelation
status_updates: learns key truth about her parentage

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 254、INT. HOSPITAL CORRIDOR, NEW ORLEANS - DAY, PRESENT
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Caroline, overwhelmed in a hospital hallway, breaks down in tears as the nurse attends to duties, then composes herself and returns to the room.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Caroline breaks down in tears in the hospital hallway after the nurse's comment, then composes herself and returns to the room.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: composes herself and resumes caregiving presence
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: emotional burden of hospitalization and caregiving
evidence_quotes: And Caroline, unable to hold it in, can't stop bitter tears from running down her face...
Caroline takes another moment and goes back into the hospital room...

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 255、INT. HOSPITAL ROOM, NEW ORLEANS - DAY, THE PRESENT
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Caroline returns, reassures Daisy she's all she has, and resumes reading a book describing a child's healthy growth.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Caroline tells Daisy 'You're all I have,' expressing emotional dependence, then resumes reading to her after a pause.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Caroline re-engages with Daisy by resuming the reading after a moment of emotional tension.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: fear of loss and isolation
evidence_quotes: CAROLINE (after a moment) You're all I have.
DAISY Read the rest of it, darling.
Caroline hesitates, then sits back down, and quietly takes up the "book."

relation_updates: emotional bond with Daisy: reinforced, reciprocal dependency

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 292、INT. HOSPITAL ROOM. NEW ORLEANS - MORNING, PRESENT
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: A hurricane rages as Caroline finds a dropped book with a train schedule and a note reading, "I'm on a train in India filled with people."
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Caroline finds a dropped book containing a train schedule and a handwritten note that reads, 'I'm on a train in India filled with people,' providing her with new physical evidence of her father's presence abroad after his disappearance.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: processing new evidence of father's continued existence
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: in possession of a written message indicating her father was alive and traveling in India
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: why he stopped writing and where he was going
evidence_quotes: And as she gets up, the "book" drops on the floor.
Caroline picks it up.
CAROLINE (CONT'D) It's a train schedule.
CAROLINE (CONT'D) "I'm on a train in India filled with people."

relation_updates: reconnection with absent father through physical artifact
status_updates: shifts from uncertainty to having tangible proof of father's journey

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 303、INT. HOSPITAL ROOM, NEW ORLEANS - MORNING, PRESENT
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Daisy watches a hummingbird battle the storm to tap at her window, symbolizing infinity; she says goodnight to Benjamin and closes her eyes, embracing peace as darkness falls.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Caroline expresses regret about not knowing her father, acknowledges a sense of connection through Daisy's words, and leaves the room as Daisy dies, signaling her transition into independent action.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: coming to terms with not having known her father while accepting the connection she now feels
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: orphaned but resolved, preparing to take independent next steps
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: what her father meant by 'I'm going...'
evidence_quotes: He started to write something else... "I'm going..." But he stopped...
I wish I had known him.
I'd better go and see what they're planning to do...
Caroline goes out of the room...

relation_updates: finalizes emotional bond with Daisy through unspoken understanding
symbolically inherits the quest from Daisy's closure
status_updates: emotionally prepares to act independently after Daisy's death

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Caroline's journey unfolds at her mother Daisy's deathbed, where she transitions from a caretaker seeking closure to an emotional inheritor of a hidden family history. As she reads Benjamin Button's journal, she confronts long-buried truths—her father's identity, her mother's regrets, and her own unfulfilled longing for love and belonging. The revelations culminate in a moment of personal resolution as Daisy dies, leaving Caroline orphaned but emotionally prepared to pursue the unfinished story of her father.

focal_character_timelines.selection_reason: Aligned with the movie's ICRP character set.
focal_character_timelines.task3_relevance: Directly aligned with the same key character used in ICRP.

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 58、EXT. THE LAWN, THE NOLAN HOUSE - DAY,
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Benjamin is captivated by Daisy's blue eyes, leaving a lasting impression on him, as Tizzy announces supper is served, ending the gathering.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: young girl making a first impression on Benjamin
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Daisy is introduced performing pirouettes on a picnic table, capturing attention with her energetic and playful behavior, which immediately draws Benjamin's notice.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: established as a memorable presence in Benjamin's life following their first encounter
evidence_quotes: A LITTLE GIRL'S VOICE * Grandma, look at me...

relation_updates: first meeting with Benjamin
status_updates: recognized for her liveliness and charm

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 62、INT. BACK STAIRWELL, NOLAN HOUSE - LATE NIGHT,
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Daisy and Benjamin are caught under sheets by Grandma Fuller, who scolds them and sends them to bed; Queenie later tells Benjamin he's different and misunderstood, while Mr. Oti silently observes him, acknowledging his struggles.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Daisy shares a personal secret with Benjamin and responds to his with affirmation, creating a moment of mutual vulnerability under the dining table.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to connect through mutual vulnerability
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: emotionally bonded to Benjamin through exchanged secrets and shared intimacy in the hidden space under the sheet
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: fear of separation and disapproval from adults after being discovered
evidence_quotes: DAISY Under here... And she ducks under a sheet, beneath the dining table... Benjamin follows her into the \"fort...\"
DAISY You are different than anybody I have ever met.

relation_updates: exchanges secrets with Benjamin
affirms Benjamin's uniqueness
status_updates: experiences emotional closeness despite age difference

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 90、EXT. BEHIND THE KITCHEN - NOLAN HOUSE - DAWN,
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Benjamin, in an old peacoat, gives Daisy a large coat and whispers if she can swim; she replies she can do anything he can, and they hurry down the street together.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: child joining Benjamin in a secretive departure
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Daisy declares she can do anything Benjamin can and agrees to leave with him, showing willingness to face the unknown together.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: commits to leaving with Benjamin on a fast, secretive journey
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: wearing an oversized coat and moving quickly down the street with Benjamin
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: destination and dangers of the journey remain unclear
evidence_quotes: DAISY I can do anything you can do...
He gives her a heavy coat...she puts it on... It's two sizes too big for her...
BENJAMIN (CONT'D) We have to go fast...
And she follows him between the houses... the two of them going quickly down the street...

relation_updates: establishes parity and mutual daring in relationship with Benjamin

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 100、EXT. THE FRONT PORCH - NOLAN HOUSE - DAY,
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Benjamin leaves for sea as Daisy runs away, overwhelmed; he watches her go, then departs, vowing to send postcards from everywhere.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Daisy runs after Benjamin as he departs, asks him to send postcards from everywhere, then flees without saying more, revealing her emotional conflict.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to stay connected to Benjamin across distance
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Daisy expresses a desire to stay in contact but is unable to fully communicate her feelings before running away.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: inability to fully articulate her emotions
evidence_quotes: When suddenly Daisy is calling him... DAISY Benjamin... Benjamin...
She comes running.
DAISY (CONT'D) Where are you going?
BENJAMIN To sea. I'll send you a postcard.

relation_updates: mutual emotional intensity with Benjamin

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 110、INT. LANDMARK BUILDING, DANCE LOFT, NEW YORK - DAY,
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Daisy performs technically proficient but uninspired dance for a committee, blending in with other trained dancers without standing out.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Daisy auditions for the School of American Ballet with technical precision but lacks emotional expression, failing to stand out among other candidates and perceiving herself as replaceable.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to succeed as a standout ballet dancer
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: discouraged and viewing herself as indistinct within the corps
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: how to express individuality and emotion through her dancing
evidence_quotes: Daisy moving with technical proficiency -- but it's bloodless, without any real distinction...
She gets nods - - but no kudos...
DAISY'S (V.O.) One of the "corps"... another dancing gypsy...
We see Daisy training... just another lithe body.

relation_updates: perceived as anonymous by selection committee
status_updates: experiences lack of recognition in competitive setting

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 159、INT. THE NOLAN HOUSE - EARLY ONE EVENING,
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Benjamin reunites with Daisy, now a woman, expressing awe and regret for stopping his letters, acknowledging her transformation and his lingering feelings.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Daisy reunites with Benjamin after years apart, initially not recognizing him, then embracing him with visible emotion, marking a shift in their relationship dynamic.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: reconnect with Benjamin and express concern for his well-being
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Daisy and Benjamin acknowledge each other's presence with surprise and warmth, their embrace suggesting changed feelings between them
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: whether their reconnection will develop into a romantic relationship remains uncertain
evidence_quotes: DAISY Oh, excuse me... is Queenie here...?
BENJAMIN Daisy... it's me, Benjamin.
DAISY Benjamin...Oh my God it's you... Of course it's you...
They embrace, and there's a moment when the touch is not just of old friends, but something different...

relation_updates: emotional shift in relationship with Benjamin from friends to romantic potential
status_updates: now a confident young woman in her early twenties

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 165、EXT. A NEW ORLEANS PARK - NIGHT
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Daisy confronts Benjamin about his reluctance, offers intimacy, but he withdraws, urging her to return to New York; she leaves, rejected, and Benjamin regrets losing his chance with her.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: seeking emotional and physical intimacy with Benjamin
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Daisy dances for Benjamin and openly expresses her desire for connection, but he does not reciprocate her advances.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to be seen, desired, and physically connected with by Benjamin
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: feeling rejected when Benjamin declines her invitation to be intimate
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Benjamin's emotional hesitation creates a barrier to their physical and emotional connection
evidence_quotes: She comes next to him and she kisses him... and when he doesn't respond...
DAISY Let's go back to the house... or get a room somewhere... we can put down your coat...
BENJAMIN It's not that I don't want to... I just can't... not tonight....

relation_updates: emotional and physical intimacy grows during dinner
status_updates: working as a maitre d' in a New Orleans restaurant

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 245、INT. DANCE STUDIO, NEW ORLEANS - NIGHT,
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Daisy and Benjamin share an intimate moment, reflecting on their age and connection; she reveals she's pregnant, moving him deeply as they embrace, marking a new chapter in their converging lives.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Daisy reveals her pregnancy to Benjamin during a quiet moment at the dance studio, deepening their emotional connection as they acknowledge their fleeting convergence in age.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: building a life with Benjamin and carrying their child
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Daisy is pregnant and emotionally connected to Benjamin, embracing their shared future while aware of its temporal limits.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: the temporary nature of their relationship due to Benjamin's reverse aging and their opposing life trajectories.
evidence_quotes: DAISY I thought I was far too old... I'm pregnant...

relation_updates: deepens bond with Benjamin through shared parenthood
auxiliary.persona_anchor: nurturing and present-focused, finding joy in love despite impermanence

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 263、INT. SUBURBAN TOWNHOUSE, NEW ORLEANS - LATE AT NIGHT,
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Daisy remains in bed after hearing a motorcycle leave, then gets up, takes her baby from the crib, and holds the child in bed under dim light.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Daisy retrieves her baby from the crib and holds her in bed after hearing Benjamin leave on a motorcycle, physically reaffirming her care.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: protect and comfort child
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Daisy assumes sole care of her child with quiet resolve following Benjamin's departure.
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: how she will explain Benjamin's absence to the child
evidence_quotes: the sound of the motorcycle driving away
She gets up.
She takes the baby out of her crib and into bed with her
holding her baby in the bare light...

relation_updates: strengthens bond with daughter in absence of father

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 278、INT. THE KITCHEN, NOLAN HOUSE - MORNING,
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Eight-year-old Benjamin, often disoriented and forgetful, becomes upset when caregivers correct him, but finds comfort in Daisy's understanding as she gently redirects him.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Daisy intervenes to calm Benjamin when he becomes enraged after being told he already ate breakfast, redirecting him with physical comfort and reassurance.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to soothe and protect Benjamin from distress
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Benjamin is physically comforted and removed from the agitated situation, reinforcing Daisy's consistent role in managing his confusion
timeline_nodes.unresolved_issue: Benjamin's worsening disorientation and memory lapses
evidence_quotes: And just then Daisy comes into the kitchen. She sees him raging.
Let's see if we can find something else for you to do.
I know you can't. And as she puts her arm around him, taking him out of the room, understanding...
And every day I would stop by... and make sure he was comfortable...

relation_updates: acts as a stabilizing presence when others cannot
status_updates: establishes herself as the only one who can de-escalate his outbursts

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 291、INT. BENJAMIN AND DAISY'S ROOM, NOLAN HOUSE - DAY,
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Benjamin closes his eyes and passes away peacefully in Daisy's lap, his stillness marking the end of his journey as she holds him.
timeline_nodes.role_in_context: emotional climax and resolution of relationship
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Daisy witnesses Benjamin's final moments and perceives his continued recognition of her, affirming their enduring connection.
timeline_nodes.goal_state: to be present with Benjamin at the end
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: Daisy remains with Benjamin after he dies, holding him peacefully
evidence_quotes: He looked into my eyes... And we see him looking into her eyes...
he moved his fingers... and with his little thumb he held my thumb...
at that moment I knew... he knew who I was...
And then he closed his eyes like he was sleeping... And we see his eyes flutter and softly close... forever.

relation_updates: final affirmation of deep emotional connection with Benjamin
status_updates: achieves emotional certainty of mutual recognition
auxiliary.persona_anchor: loving, present, at peace with loss

timeline_nodes.scene_title: 303、INT. HOSPITAL ROOM, NEW ORLEANS - MORNING, PRESENT
timeline_nodes.scene_summary: Daisy watches a hummingbird battle the storm to tap at her window, symbolizing infinity; she says goodnight to Benjamin and closes her eyes, embracing peace as darkness falls.
timeline_nodes.salient_development: Daisy says goodbye to Benjamin and dies peacefully after witnessing a hummingbird struggle against the storm.
timeline_nodes.resulting_state: at peace, life concluded
evidence_quotes: Daisy's alone now... looking out the window... at the howling, hurricane...
she watches it go...
Goodnight, Benjamin...
she closes her eyes for the very last time

relation_updates: symbolic farewell to Benjamin through the hummingbird

focal_character_timelines.timeline_summary: Daisy's life unfolds through a nonlinear narrative, tracing her emotional journey from childhood to old age, centered on her enduring connection with Benjamin Button. Her story moves between her final days in a New Orleans hospital and key moments of love, loss, motherhood, and artistic fulfillment. She evolves from a vibrant child into a resilient woman who confronts aging, heartbreak, and caregiving, ultimately finding peace in memory and legacy.
